summary: Switch the native app to an embedded MediaPipe Hand Landmarker runtime and route scrolls through the macOS HID event tap.
scope: Use MediaPipe GPU first for 21 landmarks, handedness, and two-hand detection; retain Apple Vision as fallback.
Serve the bundled WASM and model only from an in-process 127.0.0.1 server for WKWebView.
Render a mirrored calibration view, simplified display skeleton, dynamic pose labels, and a pinch midpoint dot.
Inject global HID scroll-wheel events at the focused target window so Chromium receives them.
Provide a normal Dock control window as well as the menu-bar entry.
validation: MediaPipe GPU reached ready state and returned a live left-hand pose on the installed app.
TypeScript and Swift builds, Info.plist lint, entitlements, and ad-hoc signature verification passed
notes: The final ad-hoc binary needs one Accessibility reauthorization before real Chrome scrolling can be accepted.
Cross-application direction and false-trigger rates still require user hand testing.
